title: Finding the five-number summary
problem: Find the five-number summary of the sorted data set: $2, 5, 9, 13, 17, 22, 26, 30$.

steps.working: There are 8 values, so the median is the mean of the 4th and 5th values. $$\text{Median} = \frac{13 + 17}{2} = \frac{30}{2} = 15$$
The lower half is $2, 5, 9, 13$. Q1 is the mean of the 2nd and 3rd values. $$\text{Q1} = \frac{5 + 9}{2} = \frac{14}{2} = 7$$
The upper half is $17, 22, 26, 30$. Q3 is the mean of the 2nd and 3rd values. $$\text{Q3} = \frac{22 + 26}{2} = \frac{48}{2} = 24$$

answer: Five-number summary: min $= 2$, Q1 $= 7$, median $= 15$, Q3 $= 24$, max $= 30$.

title: Finding the IQR and range from a box plot
problem: A box plot shows: minimum $= 14$, Q1 $= 28$, median $= 42$, Q3 $= 57$, maximum $= 80$. Find: (a) the range, (b) the IQR.

steps.working: The IQR of $29$ means the middle $50\%$ of data values span a range of $29$ units, from $28$ to $57$.

title: Identifying potential outliers using the IQR rule
problem: A data set has Q1 $= 22$ and Q3 $= 46$. A value of $85$ appears in the data. Determine whether $85$ is a potential outlier.

steps.instruction: Compare the value to the upper fence.
steps.working: Since $85 > 82$, the value $85$ lies beyond the upper fence.
A value is a potential outlier if it is more than $1.5 \times \text{IQR}$ beyond Q1 or Q3. Since $85 > 82$, the value $85$ is a potential outlier.

answer: Yes, $85$ is a potential outlier because it exceeds the upper fence of $82$.
